{"key_event":"1901 associations law and secular republican consolidation","reasoning":"Civil society remained very strong and broadened through legal associations, mass press, socialism, unions, and protest networks, though Catholic institutions and revolutionary syndicalists faced state pressure. The republican state gained legitimacy, secular administrative authority, oversight of associations, and demonstrated coercive capacity, despite temporary legitimacy shocks from surveillance scandals, strikes, and the Midi revolt.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":8.6,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":8.8}
